title: Start with package metadata and analysis boundaries
description: Begin in package.json to see that this stage is a Node 18 CLI built around tree-sitter and exposed through index.js. Then glance at .gitignore and .understandignore to understand which generated artifacts or noisy paths are intentionally kept out of version control and analysis.

title: Read the composition root in index.js
description: index.js is the dependency-injection root and the best mental starting point for the runtime architecture. It wires together discovery, parser registration, AST walking, extractors, and JSON output, then turns CLI arguments into a single pipeline run.

title: Follow end-to-end orchestration and file discovery
description: AnalysisPipeline.js shows the control flow for the whole stage: discover files, choose a parser, walk the parsed tree, and write accumulated facts. FileDiscovery.js explains the first filtering boundary by recursively finding only supported source files while skipping hidden folders and common build output.

title: Understand how source files become parse results
description: The parsing layer separates interface from implementation so the pipeline never hard-codes language details. Read IParser.js for the contract, ParserRegistry.js for extension-based parser selection, and TreeSitterParser.js for the concrete JavaScript/JSX parser that returns tree-sitter parse results.

title: See how parsed trees are traversed and dispatched
description: The walker layer turns one parse result into one file-level facts object. IASTWalker.js defines that contract, while TreeSitterWalker.js implements a depth-first traversal with a node-type dispatch table so only relevant extractors run for each syntax node.

title: Study the extractor model and fact categories
description: IExtractor.js defines the extension point that keeps each fact category single-purpose and pluggable. The concrete extractors then map specific tree-sitter node types into normalized architectural facts for imports, exports, functions, calls and JSX usage, and classes.

title: Finish at the output boundary and sample artifact
description: The output layer shows how extracted facts leave the pipeline without coupling orchestration to one storage format. IFactWriter.js defines the persistence contract, JsonFactWriter.js implements JSON serialization, and micro-lap-facts.json gives you a concrete example of the final per-file schema emitted by this stage.
